what_it_does: An integration/regression test that exercises the surface-intent, approval, dispatch, and operator HTTP face paths to prove that a build-intent's context bundle is resolved and persisted at consent time and that the launched build composes its prompt from that exact persisted bundle without re-resolving a fresh context; it uses a mocked launcher so no real subprocess is ever spawned.
observations: The module docstring frames the test as a design proof for the X5 consent-time resolution trust property.
It creates isolated temporary stores per scenario via fresh_suite and reloads a Suite from disk to prove persistence.
make_capturing_launcher records the decision, the repo, and the composed instruction, then raises a sentinel to short-circuit dispatch before any real launch.
It includes a positive-control mutation: a new comment is attached after approval and a fresh gather proves it would be included if re-resolved.
It inspects the source of impl.launch and build_instruction for the absence of _r2_gather and resolve_scope.
A real HTTP face sub-test spins runtime.bridge on an ephemeral port and reads payload.context via GET /api/surfaced.
It asserts safe-by-default wire state: permission_mode() == plan and wire_armed() is False.
A faulthandler hang-guard is installed with a 60-second exit timeout.
standouts: The consent-time freezing proof with a positive-control post-approval mutation.
The mocked capturing launcher that raises a sentinel to guarantee no real subprocess.
The end-to-end operator HTTP face retrieval of the resolved context bundle.
conventions: Uses a global PASS counter with check() and raises() helpers.
Creates temporary FsStore roots and cleans up via fresh_suite.
Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN and stubs the default suite runner.
Reloads fabric.config and runtime.bridge for an isolated HTTP face test.
Treats the module docstring as a design manifesto and regression lock.
concerns: raises() catches the generic Exception class.
Source-code string checks for _r2_gather and resolve_scope are brittle to refactoring.
The HTTP face test mutates os.environ and reloads modules, which can affect global state.
Direct use of internal-looking _r2_gather and _r2_score_and_cap for the positive control.
faulthandler.dump_traceback_later(..., exit=True) will abort the whole process on a hang.
notes: A regression test that locks the surfaced==launched consent-time freezing property using a mocked launcher and a real operator HTTP read face.
questions: Do Suite.dispatch_decision and resolve_surfaced expose exactly the signatures assumed here?
Does _LaunchReached propagate through dispatch_decision because only LaunchError is caught?
Is the HTTP server thread shutdown race-free under all failure modes?
Is the _r2_score_and_cap signature stable across future refactors?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to prove and guard the X5 consent-time resolution property, and the file matches that purpose by minting, persisting, mutating live context, dispatching with a mock, and asserting the launched bundle stays frozen.
intention_signals: To make the trust property explicit, enforce it through a named regression test, and show that the operator can read the exact approved context over the real HTTP face before launch.
novelty: A test file written as a design proof with a HARD LAW mocked launcher, a positive-control mutation step, source-code inspection for purity, and a live HTTP read-path sub-test.
contribution: A regression test that documents and guards the consent-time resolution invariant, a reusable capturing-launcher pattern for safe dispatch testing, and an end-to-end HTTP read-path check for the approval surface.
summary_for_embedding: A standalone regression test for the X5 consent-time resolution property that verifies a build-intent's context bundle is persisted at mint, unchanged by later live-context mutations, and retrievable via the operator HTTP face, using a mocked launcher to avoid subprocesses.
intention_for_embedding: To lock the invariant that the context an operator approves is exactly the context the launched build composes from, with no re-resolution at launch.

symbols.does: Test helper that asserts a condition and increments the global PASS counter.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) is the basic assertion helper used throughout the script; it fails with a labeled message if cond is false and otherwise increments PASS and prints ok.

symbols.does: Test helper that expects a callable to raise a given exception.
symbols.description: raises(label, fn, exc=Exception) runs fn, increments PASS if the expected exception is raised, and fails if no exception occurs.

symbols.does: Creates an isolated Suite with a temporary FsStore and a stubbed suite runner.
symbols.description: fresh_suite() builds a new temporary store root, discovers nodes, and returns a Suite whose default suite runner is stubbed so no real subprocess can spawn.

symbols.does: Sentinel exception used by the mocked launcher to short-circuit dispatch.
symbols.description: class _LaunchReached(Exception) is raised by the fake launcher after recording the decision; because dispatch_decision only catches LaunchError, the sentinel propagates and proves launch was reached without a real subprocess.

symbols.does: Factory that returns a capturing dict and a fake launch closure.
symbols.description: make_capturing_launcher() returns a captured dictionary and a fake_launch function that records the decision, repo, and composed instruction before raising _LaunchReached.

symbols.does: Mock launcher that records the dispatch decision and composed prompt, then short-circuits.
symbols.description: fake_launch(decision, repo) stores the decision and repo, computes the instruction via build_instruction(decision), and raises _LaunchReached so no real claude -p subprocess is spawned.

symbols.does: Approves a surfaced build-intent and returns the resolve event sequence.
symbols.description: approve_seq(suite, sid) calls resolve_surfaced with approve and scans the store event log for the matching resolve event to obtain the seq needed for dispatch.

symbols.does: Drives dispatch_decision with the capturing launcher and returns the captured payload.
symbols.description: drive_capture(suite, sid, seq) installs the fake launcher, calls dispatch_decision, catches the _LaunchReached sentinel, and returns the captured decision and instruction.

symbols.does: HTTP POST helper for the real operator-face sub-test.
symbols.description: _post(p, body) sends a JSON POST to the local bridge server on the ephemeral port and returns the status and parsed response body.

symbols.does: HTTP GET helper for the real operator-face sub-test.
symbols.description: _get(p) sends a GET to the local bridge server on the ephemeral port and returns the status and parsed response body.
